title: CDI Proteomics Analysis System
text:
CDI Proteomics Analysis System
Results-First Edition
This guide introduces a practical system for interpreting proteomics results after receiving processed output tables from a proteomics facility, collaborator, or vendor.
The focus is on moving from differential protein abundance results to biological interpretation.
Proteomics result tables
        ↓
Differential abundance results
        ↓
Protein filtering and ranking
        ↓
Identifier cleaning and annotation
        ↓
GO and pathway enrichment
        ↓
STRING network analysis
        ↓
Biological interpretation
        ↓
Reproducible report

title: Preface and Overview
text:
Results-First Approach
Proteomics was one of the first omics fields I worked with, especially through protein-level interpretation and GO functional annotation.
This system is both a return to that foundation and a refreshed, reproducible guide for modern proteomics result interpretation.
Many researchers do not begin proteomics analysis from raw mass spectrometry files. They often receive processed result files that include protein abundance tables, log2 fold-changes, p-values, and adjusted p-values.
This guide begins from that practical starting point.

title: Preface and Overview
text:
Raw mass spectrometry files
        ↓
Processed by facility / vendor / collaborator
        ↓
Protein abundance table
        ↓
Differential protein abundance table
        ↓
Interpretation system

title: Preface and Overview
text:
System Goal
The goal is to move from thousands of differentially abundant proteins to clear biological themes, candidate proteins, pathways, networks, and a reproducible report.

title: Proteomics Results Landscape
text:
Common Files
This chapter introduces common proteomics result types and how they fit into downstream analysis.
Proteomics projects may produce:

title: Proteomics Results Landscape
text:
raw mass spectrometry files
peptide-level tables
protein-level abundance tables
differential abundance result tables
sample metadata
quality control summaries
annotation files

title: Proteomics Results Landscape
text:
Results-First Entry Point
This system begins with the files most researchers commonly receive after core facility processing:
protein_id
gene_symbol
protein_name
log2FC
pvalue
padj
comparison

title: Understanding Proteomics Output Files
text:
Protein Abundance Table
This chapter explains the key result files used in downstream proteomics interpretation.
The abundance table contains quantified protein-level values across samples.

title: Understanding Proteomics Output Files
text:
Differential Result Table
The differential result table summarizes changes between conditions.
Common columns include:
protein_id
gene_symbol
protein_name
log2FC
pvalue
padj
comparison

title: Understanding Proteomics Output Files
text:
Key Interpretation Columns

log2FC: direction and magnitude of change
pvalue: statistical evidence before multiple testing correction
padj: adjusted p-value or false discovery rate
protein_id: protein identifier
gene_symbol: mapped gene symbol

title: Sample Metadata and Comparison Design
text:
Sample Metadata
Proteomics interpretation depends on knowing which samples belong to which biological groups.
A minimal metadata table should describe:
crumbs: 5  Sample Metadata and Comparison Design

title: Sample Metadata and Comparison Design
text:
sample_id
condition
group
replicate
batch
comparison
crumbs: 5  Sample Metadata and Comparison Design

title: Sample Metadata and Comparison Design
text:
Comparison Design
Before interpreting differential proteins, define the biological comparison clearly.
Examples:
treated_vs_control
disease_vs_healthy
timepoint_24h_vs_0h
resistant_vs_sensitive
crumbs: 5  Sample Metadata and Comparison Design

title: Quality Control of Result Tables
text:
Table-Level Checks
This chapter checks whether the received result tables are suitable for interpretation.
Recommended checks include:
crumbs: 6  Quality Control of Result Tables

title: Quality Control of Result Tables
text:
required columns are present
protein identifiers are not missing
log2FC values are numeric
p-values and adjusted p-values are numeric
duplicate protein IDs are handled
comparison labels are clear
crumbs: 6  Quality Control of Result Tables

title: Quality Control of Result Tables
text:
QC Principle
Before interpreting biology, confirm that the result table is internally consistent.
crumbs: 6  Quality Control of Result Tables

title: Differential Protein Abundance Results
text:
Basic Result Interpretation
Differential protein abundance analysis identifies proteins that differ between biological conditions.

title: Differential Protein Abundance Results
text:
positive log2FC  → higher in numerator condition
negative log2FC  → lower in numerator condition
small padj       → stronger statistical evidence

title: Differential Protein Abundance Results
text:
Significant Differentially Abundant Proteins
A common starting threshold is:
padj < 0.05
Optional effect-size filtering may include:
abs(log2FC) >= 1

title: Ranking and Filtering Differentially Abundant Proteins
text:
Core Outputs
This chapter turns a large differential result table into prioritized protein lists.
crumbs: 8  Ranking and Filtering Differentially Abundant Proteins

title: Ranking and Filtering Differentially Abundant Proteins
text:
all differential proteins
significant DEP
top upregulated proteins
top downregulated proteins
ranked protein list
crumbs: 8  Ranking and Filtering Differentially Abundant Proteins

title: Ranking and Filtering Differentially Abundant Proteins
text:
Ranking Strategy
Proteins can be ranked by:

adjusted p-value
absolute log2 fold-change
direction of change
biological relevance
presence in known pathways or protein families
crumbs: 8  Ranking and Filtering Differentially Abundant Proteins

title: Protein Identifier Cleaning and Annotation
text:
Common Identifiers
Protein identifiers must be cleaned and mapped before enrichment or network analysis.
Proteomics tables may contain:
crumbs: 9  Protein Identifier Cleaning and Annotation

title: Protein Identifier Cleaning and Annotation
text:
UniProt accession
UniProt protein name
gene symbol
Ensembl gene ID
RefSeq accession
protein description
crumbs: 9  Protein Identifier Cleaning and Annotation

title: Protein Identifier Cleaning and Annotation
text:
Why Identifier Cleaning Matters
Incorrect or inconsistent identifiers can affect:

GO enrichment
pathway enrichment
STRING network mapping
candidate protein prioritization
crumbs: 9  Protein Identifier Cleaning and Annotation

title: Functional Enrichment and GO Annotation
text:
GO Domains
GO functional annotation is a central part of proteomics interpretation.
Gene Ontology includes:
crumbs: 10  Functional Enrichment and GO Annotation

title: Functional Enrichment and GO Annotation
text:
Biological Process
Molecular Function
Cellular Component
crumbs: 10  Functional Enrichment and GO Annotation

title: Functional Enrichment and GO Annotation
text:
Enrichment Workflow
significant protein list
        ↓
identifier mapping
        ↓
background definition
        ↓
GO enrichment
        ↓
pathway enrichment
        ↓
biological interpretation
crumbs: 10  Functional Enrichment and GO Annotation

title: Functional Enrichment and GO Annotation
text:
Important Principle
Functional enrichment should be interpreted in relation to the original biological question, not as a standalone list of terms.
crumbs: 10  Functional Enrichment and GO Annotation

title: STRING Network Analysis
text:
Network Analysis Goals
STRING can be used to explore protein-protein association networks among differentially abundant proteins.
STRING analysis can help identify:

title: STRING Network Analysis
text:
connected protein modules
biological processes represented by protein clusters
hub proteins
pathway-level structure
candidate proteins for follow-up

title: STRING Network Analysis
text:
Input Lists
Recommended inputs:
all significant DEP
upregulated DEP
downregulated DEP
top ranked proteins

title: Visualizing Proteomics Results
text:
Core Figures
Visualizations help summarize differential abundance and interpretation results.
Recommended figures include:

title: Visualizing Proteomics Results
text:
volcano plot
top proteins barplot
heatmap
GO enrichment dotplot
pathway enrichment plot
STRING network figure

title: Visualizing Proteomics Results
text:
Visualization Principle
Figures should support interpretation, not only decorate the report.

title: Biological Interpretation System
text:
Interpretation Questions
This chapter turns tables, figures, GO terms, pathways, and networks into a biological story.
Ask:

title: Biological Interpretation System
text:
Which proteins changed?
In which direction?
How strong is the change?
Are proteins functionally related?
Which GO terms or pathways are enriched?
Do networks reveal protein modules?
How do the findings answer the biological question?

title: Biological Interpretation System
text:
From Results to Insight
DEP table
        ↓
ranked proteins
        ↓
GO terms
        ↓
pathways
        ↓
network modules
        ↓
biological themes
        ↓
reportable findings

title: Reproducible Proteomics Report
text:
Report Sections
The final output of the system is a reproducible report.
A practical proteomics interpretation report may include:

title: Reproducible Proteomics Report
text:
study objective
sample metadata
comparison design
data inputs
quality control
differential abundance summary
top proteins
GO enrichment
pathway enrichment
STRING network analysis
biological interpretation
limitations
next steps

title: Reproducible Proteomics Report
text:
Report Outputs
HTML report
tables
figures
reusable input lists
analysis log

title: Raw Data Expansion Roadmap
text:
Future Expansion
This Results-First Edition begins from processed proteomics results.
A future Raw Data Edition can expand backward into raw mass spectrometry processing.

title: Raw Data Expansion Roadmap
text:
RAW files
        ↓
spectral processing
        ↓
peptide identification
        ↓
protein inference
        ↓
protein quantification
        ↓
differential abundance analysis
        ↓
results-first interpretation workflow

title: Raw Data Expansion Roadmap
text:
Possible Future Topics

MaxQuant

DIA-NN
Spectronaut-style outputs
peptide-spectrum matches
protein inference
